Pretax Return on Net Operating Assets: Trailing 12-month operating income after depreciation divided by average net operating assets
15/36 Week Stock Price Ratio: Moving average of last 15 weeks' prices divided by moving average of last 36 weeks' prices

5-year Relative TTM Dividend Yield: Current dividend yield minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation

5-year Relative TTM Free Cash Flow-to-Price: Current TTM free cash flow-to-price minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation

5-year Relative TTM Sales-to-Price: Current TTM sales-to-price ratio minus 60-month average, scaled by 60-month standard deviation

Return on Equity: TTM income before extra items divided by average common equity in same period

60-Month Beta : It is defined as 0.67 times the 60-month beta plus 0.33Beta is the covariance between a stock's monthly return and the monthly S&P 500 index return in the prior 60-month period divided by the variance of the monthly S&P 500 index return in the same period.
Product of the adjusted 60-month Beta (market sensitivity) and the 60-month Sigma (volatility of monthly price returns), a combined indicator of risk
Book Leverage ratio: Total assets from most recent quarter divided by book equity, reflecting company capital structure
Liquidity measure: Most recent quarter’s cash and equivalents divided by current liabilities
Current Cash Flow Debt Coverage Ratio: Most recent quarterly cash flow from operations minus dividends, divided by current interest-bearing debt; ability to cover debt
Cash Flow Leverage: Total liabilities divided by trailing 12-month operating cash flow, a stress test of cash flow coverage
Slope of least squares trend line of last 60 months’ monthly trading volume versus time; measures long-term volume trend
Current ratio: Current assets divided by current liabilities (most recent quarter), indicating short-term financial health

Dividend coverage ratio: company's trailing annual operating earnings divided by annual dividends; reflects ability to pay dividends from earnings
Flow ratio: (current assets - cash & equivalents) divided by (current liabilities - short-term debt), measuring liquidity after excluding most liquid assets and debts
Capital gearing ratio: long-term debt divided by (total assets minus current liability); measures leverage excluding current liabilities

Visibility Ratio: recent daily volume divided by average daily volume over prior 50 days (trading activity indicator)
Visibility Ratio; most recent daily trading volume divided by average daily trading volume in prior 50 trading days
Average trading volume over the preceding 6 months (trading activity measure)
6-month moving average of average monthly turnover ratio; measures trading volume normalized by shares outstanding, averaged over the past 6 months
Capital strength module, a composite score measuring a company's financial strength and capital adequacy
A composite/module summarizing capital strength factors (e.g. leverage, capital ratios)
Cash burn rate, indicating the rate at which a company is spending its cash reserves
Rate at which a company spends cash, e.g. negative free cash flow in a period
Six-month alpha change rank, ranking stocks by change in alpha (excess return over benchmark) over the latest six months
Ranked change in alpha (risk-adjusted excess return) over a six-month period
Change in operating liability leverage, measuring how a company's operating liabilities leverage has changed over a given period
Change in operating liability leverage over a given period
Percentage change in shares outstanding, tracking how the total number of a company's shares has changed over time
Percent change in shares outstanding over a specific period

A custom composite signal designed for small-cap stocks, combining multiple factors

Industry-relative core earnings per share to price ratio, comparing normalized/core EPS-to-price relative to peers in the same industry
Industry-relative core earnings per share to price ratio
Industry-relative leading (estimated forward) EPS-to-price ratio, comparing a stock's forward earnings to price, relative to industry peers
Industry-relative leading 4 quarters' estimated EPS to price ratio
Industry-relative free cash flow to price ratio, measuring a company's free cash flow compared to price, adjusted relative to its industry peers
Industry-relative ratio of free cash flow to price
Industry-relative return on equity, comparing a company's ROE to its industry average
Industry-relative return on equity; how a stock's ROE compares to its industry

Short interest ratio, measuring the relative amount of shares shorted compared to available shares or average daily volume
Ratio of shares sold short to total shares outstanding or float
Street revision confidence, measuring analyst confidence or consensus in recent earnings or revenue estimate revisions
Confidence in street (analyst) earnings revisions, likely a score or rank
Standardized unexpected cash flow, indicating the magnitude of cash flow that deviated from expectations, standardized across the universe
Standardized measure of unexpected (surprise) cash flow, normalized across stocks

Return on Equity; trailing 12-month income before extraordinary items divided by average common equity during the same period

Trailing Twelve Month Free Cash Flow-to-Price; trailing 12-month free cash flow per share divided by month-end trading price

Abnormal return around quarterly earnings release; measures stock's excess return in the event-window of earnings reports, likely relative to normal or market return
Book Leverage: most recently reported quarterly total assets divided by book equity, indicating the leverage ratio using book values
Capital expenditure-to-total assets: trailing 12-month capital expenditures divided by total assets from most recent quarter
Cash burn rate: negative of (cash from operations plus cash from investments) divided by (cash plus short-term investments); indicates rate at which company uses its liquidity
Industry relative book-to-market ratio: stock's book-to-price ratio minus industry average, deflated by standard deviation for industry
Industry relative trailing 12-month EBITDA-to-price ratio: stock's EBITDA-to-price minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Industry relative trailing 12-month EPS-to-price ratio: stock's EPS-to-price minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation for EPS-to-price
Industry relative trailing 12-month free cash flow-to-price ratio: stock's FCF-to-price minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Industry relative trailing 12-month sales-to-price ratio: stock's TTM sales-to-price minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation
Trailing 12-month pro forma earnings divided by trading price; earnings-to-price ratio using pro forma (adjusted) earnings
Skewness of the distribution of analysts' leading 12-month earnings per share (EPS) estimates for a stock; measures asymmetry in analyst forecasts
Industry-adjusted doubtful account receivables: asset-adjusted annual doubtful receivables minus industry mean, deflated by industry standard deviation
4-week industry relative return: stock's 4-week return minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation of 4-week returns
5-day industry relative return: stock's return in last 5 days minus industry average, deflated by industry standard deviation of these returns
Ratio of 50-day moving average price to 200-day moving average price; a measure of short-term vs long-term momentum
Growth in total assets per share over the past year, comparing the latest reported quarterly total assets to those of four quarters ago
Current liabilities per share divided by closing price; measures proportion of liabilities relative to market value per share
Ratio of 15-week moving average price to 36-week moving average price for a stock; momentum indicator over medium and long-term periods
R&D intensity: average research and development expenses over trailing 12 months divided by total sales in the same period
Reinvestment rate: trailing 12-month earnings per share before extra items, less trailing 12-month dividends per share by ex-date, divided by average book equity per share in the same period
Return on Invested Capital: trailing 12-month net income and interest expense divided by average invested capital (common equity, long-term debt, minority interest, preferred stock)
Standardized unexpected cash flow; unexpected component of cash flow, likely measured relative to expectations or historical trend and standardized
Standardized unexpected earnings; measure of surprise in reported earnings relative to analyst forecasts, standardized by historical volatility
Tobin's Q: market value of equity plus debt divided by total assets; assesses whether market values firm's assets higher or lower than their cost
Accounting accruals: trailing 12-month net income minus trailing 12-month operating cash flow, scaled by beginning total assets; measures quality of earnings

TTM sales-to-enterprise value: trailing 12-month sales divided by most recent enterprise value (EV: market value of equity plus long-term/short-term debt, preferred stock, minority interest, minus cash & equivalents)
Unexpected change in accounts receivable: actual change in accounts receivable minus expected (prior year's closing balance multiplied by trailing 12-month sales growth), scaled by total assets
Visibility ratio: most recent daily trading volume divided by average daily trading volume over previous 50 days; measures trading activity relative to norm
Year-over-year change in leverage: difference between most recent quarterly total liabilities as a percentage of total assets and the same percentage one year ago
